data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7dc6a1d0">
    <title>investigate why pre-merge checks blocked all 4 attempts on fizeau-bd5193ca</title>
    <description>
Context:
On 2026-05-08, bead `fizeau-bd5193ca` ("Fix fiz version JSON output") in the fizeau repo went through 4 successive `ddx work` execute-bead attempts:
- 20260508T175640-b12741e5 (15m, $2.59, sonnet) → task_succeeded → result preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/…/2c3f896a
- 20260508T181204-20d01e6d (18m, $2.68, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as b673f48a
- 20260508T183001-483c8a6e (15m, $1.74, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as ee719835
- 20260508T184500-34ccc16f (~10m, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as ebbeb0ad

None merged onto master; only `chore: update tracker (execute-bead …)` commits landed. Bead stayed in_progress until the loop was killed manually.

Operator manually cherry-picked attempt 3 (ee719835) onto fizeau master as 44d85287 and verified all acceptance criteria pass:
- `go test ./cmd/fiz ./agentcli` → both packages OK
- `fiz --version` → human-readable with non-empty commit
- `fiz version --json` → valid JSON with non-empty version, non-empty commit, boolean dirty

So the implementations themselves were correct. Some pre-merge check blocked them anyway. We don't yet know which check or why — see ddx-62edcb10 for the orthogonal observability bug that hid the rejection.

Investigation goals:
1. Read `RunPreMergeChecks` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:148-area) and enumerate every check it runs by default for a fizeau-style repo.
2. Replay one of the preserved iteration refs (e.g. `refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/20260508T183001-483c8a6e-e9e33bba16bb`) through the pre-merge check pipeline against the matching base_rev to reproduce the block locally.
3. Identify which check returned Blocked=true and why. Determine whether it was a real defect in the implementation (and the cherry-pick happened to skip the check) or a false positive in the check itself.
4. If false positive, file a follow-up bead to fix the check; if real defect, document why the cherry-pick still passed go test.

Useful artifacts (in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau):
- `.ddx/executions/20260508T17{5640,1204},18{3001,4500}-*/result.json` — worker outputs
- `refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/*` — preserved result trees
- `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-2026-05.jsonl` — session metadata
- `.ddx/attachments/fizeau-bd5193ca/events.jsonl` — overflow events

Depends on: ddx-62edcb10 only insofar as fixing that bug would have made this investigation a one-line lookup instead of a code dive. They can be tackled independently.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Investigation report identifies the specific pre-merge check (by name/file:line) that returned Blocked for at least one of the four attempts on fizeau-bd5193ca.
2. Report explains the failure (real defect vs false-positive in the check itself).
3. If false positive: a follow-up bead is filed against the offending check.
4. If real defect: report explains why the operator's manual cherry-pick of ee719835 still passes `go test ./cmd/fiz ./agentcli`.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:pre-merge-checks, kind:investigation</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="8f0513a20995fdbb82680a7c31d011902e45bacb" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Investigation complete. Root cause established, report committed, follow-up bead filed.

**Findings:**

- **Attempt 1** was a real merge conflict: commit `ac79cd13` was pushed to master while the attempt ran, so `currentTip ≠ baseRev` → merge path → conflict → preserved. This was correct behavior.

- **Attempts 2–4** were all preserved by **evidence commit failure** caused by fizeau's `.gitignore` covering `.ddx/executions/`. The chain: `StageDir` runs `git add` without `--force` → silently stages nothing on gitignored paths → `CommitStaged` returns `sha=""` → `evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles` returns false → `landEvidence` errors → `preserveAfterEvidenceFailure` rolls back master and creates preserve ref. The implementation commits were valid and correct throughout.

- **Escalation exhaustion**: `ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult` maps evidence-commit-failure preserves to `"merge conflict"` → `"land_conflict"` (escalatable), so the system escalated min_power 5→7→8→9 until routing had no viable candidate.

- **Operator cherry-pick**: Worked because the code changes were valid; `go test` has no awareness of DDx evidence infrastructure.

**Follow-up bead `fizeau-af9d5f1c`**: Fix `StageDir` to use `git add --force`, and classify evidence-commit-failure preserves as non-escalatable in `execute_bead_status.go`.
